skill.name: error-handling-patterns
skill.description: Apply practical error handling patterns across Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go, including exceptions, Result types, retries, circuit breakers, and graceful degradation.
skill.summary: Design resilient application error handling with language-specific and universal patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are in Markdown documentation and code examples. No executable scripts, prompt injection attempts, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static external command detections are Markdown fences
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The Ruby or shell backtick detections point to Markdown code fence delimiters around educational examples, not executable command invocation by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown fenced code blocks or inline resource references. I found no executable shell, Ruby backtick execution, or skill script that would run these examples.

low_findings.title: Network calls are illustrative examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The detected requests.get and fetch calls appear inside example code that teaches retry and async error handling, not in executable marketplace skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both calls are embedded in documentation examples and use generic function parameters. No hardcoded external endpoint, credential forwarding, or autonomous network behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto and reconnaissance detections are substring matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines discuss designing APIs, error codes, broad exception handling, resource titles, or user identifiers, not cryptography or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, system discovery, or environment inspection logic. The scanner appears to have matched benign words and identifiers in prose or examples.

content.value_statement: Error handling often grows inconsistent across services and languages. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for resilient, debuggable application behavior.
actual_capabilities: Explains exception, Result, error code, and Option style tradeoffs.
Provides Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go error handling examples.
Guides retry logic, exponential backoff, and circuit breaker design.
Shows graceful degradation and fallback patterns for failed dependencies.
Helps review user-facing and developer-facing error messages.
Lists common pitfalls such as broad catches and swallowed errors.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not automated static analysis.
It does not verify production logs, runtime behavior, or service health.
Examples may need adaptation for local frameworks and libraries.
It does not replace security review for sensitive failure paths.

use_cases.title: Design API Error Contracts
use_cases.description: Create clear error classes, status codes, and response rules for a new service.

use_cases.title: Improve Production Reliability
use_cases.description: Add retry, fallback, and circuit breaker behavior around unstable dependencies.

use_cases.title: Teach Error Handling Patterns
use_cases.description: Compare language-specific approaches when onboarding developers or reviewing code.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Error Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an error handling strategy for this feature. Compare exceptions, Result types, and validation errors for my use case.

prompt_templates.title: Review Error Messages
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving existing user and developer messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these error messages for clarity, actionability, and safe detail. Suggest better user-facing and log-facing versions.

prompt_templates.title: Add Retry and Fallbacks
prompt_templates.scenario: Hardening an integration with an unreliable dependency
prompt_templates.prompt: Design retry, timeout, fallback, and circuit breaker behavior for this external service integration. Include failure modes and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Standardize Cross-Language Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cross-language error handling standard for Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go services. Include propagation, wrapping, observability, and API boundaries.

output_examples.input: A payment service sometimes times out during checkout.
output: Classify timeout as a recoverable dependency failure.
Use bounded retries with timeouts and jitter.
Return a clear checkout status instead of a raw dependency error.
Record structured logs with order context and no sensitive payment data.

output_examples.input: An API returns inconsistent validation errors across endpoints.
output: Define one validation error shape for all endpoints.
Separate user-facing messages from developer diagnostics.
Include stable error codes for client behavior.
Add tests for missing, malformed, and conflicting inputs.

output_examples.input: A team wants to avoid swallowed errors in async tasks.
output: Require every async boundary to return or report failures.
Add centralized logging for unexpected task failures.
Document when fallback behavior is acceptable.
Verify that monitoring alerts on repeated failures.

best_practices: Classify errors before choosing exceptions, Result types, retries, or fallbacks.
Preserve useful context while avoiding sensitive data in messages and logs.
Test failure paths with the same care as successful paths.
anti_patterns: Do not catch broad exceptions without handling, logging, or rethrowing them.
Do not expose internal stack traces or dependency details to end users.
Do not retry every failure without limits, timeouts, or idempotency checks.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with examples and implementation guidance.

faq.question: Which languages does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go examples.

faq.question: Can it help with API design?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide error contracts, status codes, and client-facing messages.

faq.question: Does it include reliability patterns?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers retries, exponential backoff, circuit breakers, and graceful degradation.

faq.question: Can it replace incident review?
faq.answer: No. It can structure error handling decisions, but humans must review production incidents.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
